triage_stuck_ros.description: Finds all ROs that are currently stuck and not progressing through the pipeline. Ranks by severity using health score and time stuck.

parameter_notes.state: US state code e.g. 'KS', 'FL', 'TX'. Pass 'ALL' to get stuck ROs across all states.

sql_template.by_state: SELECT RO_ID, ORG_NM, CNT_STATE, LOB, LATEST_STAGE_NM, FILE_STATUS_CD, IS_STUCK, IS_FAILED, FAILURE_STATUS, DART_GEN_DURATION, AVG_DART_GENERATION_DURATION, PRE_PROCESSING_HEALTH, MAPPING_APROVAL_HEALTH, ISF_GEN_HEALTH, DART_GEN_HEALTH, DART_UI_VALIDATION_HEALTH, SPS_LOAD_HEALTH, health_score, rejection_rate, failure_rate FROM ros WHERE IS_STUCK = 1 AND CNT_STATE = '{state}' ORDER BY health_score ASC, DART_GEN_DURATION DESC
sql_template.all_states: SELECT RO_ID, ORG_NM, CNT_STATE, LOB, LATEST_STAGE_NM, FILE_STATUS_CD, IS_STUCK, IS_FAILED, FAILURE_STATUS, DART_GEN_DURATION, AVG_DART_GENERATION_DURATION, PRE_PROCESSING_HEALTH, MAPPING_APROVAL_HEALTH, ISF_GEN_HEALTH, DART_GEN_HEALTH, DART_UI_VALIDATION_HEALTH, SPS_LOAD_HEALTH, health_score, rejection_rate, failure_rate FROM ros WHERE IS_STUCK = 1 ORDER BY health_score ASC, DART_GEN_DURATION DESC

triage_stuck_ros.interpretation_guidance: For each stuck RO, compare DART_GEN_DURATION against AVG_DART_GENERATION_DURATION. If duration > 2x average, flag as critical anomaly. Check FAILURE_STATUS for root cause. Distinguish IS_STUCK (stalled) from IS_FAILED (broken).

record_quality_audit.description: Computes rejection and failure rates per organization for a given state or across all states. Identifies orgs submitting non-conformant data.

parameter_notes.state: US state code e.g. 'KS', 'FL', 'TX'. Pass 'ALL' to audit all states.

sql_template.by_state: SELECT ORG_NM, CNT_STATE, LOB, SRC_SYS, COUNT(RO_ID) as total_ros, SUM(CASE WHEN IS_STUCK=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as stuck_ros, AVG(rejection_rate) as avg_rejection_rate, AVG(failure_rate) as avg_failure_rate, AVG(health_score) as avg_health_score, dominant_problem, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT FAILURE_STATUS) as failure_reasons FROM ros WHERE CNT_STATE = '{state}' GROUP BY ORG_NM, CNT_STATE, LOB ORDER BY avg_rejection_rate DESC
sql_template.all_states: SELECT ORG_NM, CNT_STATE, LOB, SRC_SYS, COUNT(RO_ID) as total_ros, SUM(CASE WHEN IS_STUCK=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as stuck_ros, AVG(rejection_rate) as avg_rejection_rate, AVG(failure_rate) as avg_failure_rate, AVG(health_score) as avg_health_score, dominant_problem, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT FAILURE_STATUS) as failure_reasons FROM ros GROUP BY ORG_NM, CNT_STATE, LOB ORDER BY avg_rejection_rate DESC

record_quality_audit.interpretation_guidance: Focus on orgs where avg_rejection_rate > 0.30 — these orgs are submitting non-conformant data. Check dominant_problem: if 'compliance' then REJ_REC_CNT > FAIL_REC_CNT meaning the org is the problem, not the pipeline. Cross-reference LOB — Medicaid FFS orgs have highest compliance burden.

market_health_report.description: Cross-layer correlation report. Joins market success metrics with file-level RO pipeline data to identify which orgs and files are driving market-level failures.

parameter_notes.state: US state code matching MARKET column in markets table e.g. 'KS', 'FL'. Pass 'ALL' for all markets.
parameter_notes.month: Month to filter on matching MONTH column format in markets table. Pass 'ALL' for all months.

sql_template.by_state_and_month: SELECT m.MARKET, m.MONTH, m.SCS_PERCENT, m.below_threshold, m.retry_lift, m.FIRST_ITER_SCS_CNT, m.NEXT_ITER_SCS_CNT, m.OVERALL_SCS_CNT, m.OVERALL_FAIL_CNT, COUNT(r.RO_ID) as active_ros, AVG(r.rejection_rate) as avg_rejection_rate, AVG(r.failure_rate) as avg_failure_rate, SUM(CASE WHEN r.IS_STUCK=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as stuck_ros, AVG(r.health_score) as avg_health_score, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT r.FAILURE_STATUS) as failure_reasons, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT r.LOB) as affected_lobs, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT r.ORG_NM) as contributing_orgs FROM markets m LEFT JOIN ros r ON r.CNT_STATE = m.MARKET WHERE m.MARKET = '{state}' AND m.MONTH = '{month}' GROUP BY m.MARKET, m.MONTH
sql_template.by_state_all_months: SELECT m.MARKET, m.MONTH, m.SCS_PERCENT, m.below_threshold, m.retry_lift, m.FIRST_ITER_SCS_CNT, m.NEXT_ITER_SCS_CNT, m.OVERALL_SCS_CNT, m.OVERALL_FAIL_CNT, COUNT(r.RO_ID) as active_ros, AVG(r.rejection_rate) as avg_rejection_rate, AVG(r.failure_rate) as avg_failure_rate, SUM(CASE WHEN r.IS_STUCK=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as stuck_ros, AVG(r.health_score) as avg_health_score, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT r.FAILURE_STATUS) as failure_reasons, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT r.LOB) as affected_lobs, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT r.ORG_NM) as contributing_orgs FROM markets m LEFT JOIN ros r ON r.CNT_STATE = m.MARKET WHERE m.MARKET = '{state}' GROUP BY m.MARKET, m.MONTH ORDER BY m.MONTH DESC
sql_template.all_markets: SELECT m.MARKET, m.MONTH, m.SCS_PERCENT, m.below_threshold, m.retry_lift, m.OVERALL_SCS_CNT, m.OVERALL_FAIL_CNT, COUNT(r.RO_ID) as active_ros, AVG(r.rejection_rate) as avg_rejection_rate, SUM(CASE WHEN r.IS_STUCK=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as stuck_ros, AVG(r.health_score) as avg_health_score FROM markets m LEFT JOIN ros r ON r.CNT_STATE = m.MARKET GROUP BY m.MARKET, m.MONTH ORDER BY m.SCS_PERCENT ASC

market_health_report.interpretation_guidance: Start with markets where below_threshold=1. For each below-threshold market, look at avg_rejection_rate of contributing ROs. If avg_rejection_rate > 0.30, source data quality is the primary driver. If stuck_ros > 0, pipeline bottleneck is also contributing. Check retry_lift — if high, retries are helping but not solving the root cause.

retry_effectiveness_analysis.description: Measures whether reprocessing and retries are actually improving success rates. Compares first-pass success against post-retry success at both market and RO level.

parameter_notes.state: US state code. Pass 'ALL' for all states.

sql_template.market_level_by_state: SELECT m.MARKET, m.MONTH, m.FIRST_ITER_SCS_CNT, m.FIRST_ITER_FAIL_CNT, m.NEXT_ITER_SCS_CNT, m.NEXT_ITER_FAIL_CNT, m.OVERALL_SCS_CNT, m.OVERALL_FAIL_CNT, m.SCS_PERCENT, m.retry_lift, ROUND(CAST(m.FIRST_ITER_SCS_CNT AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(m.FIRST_ITER_SCS_CNT + m.FIRST_ITER_FAIL_CNT, 0) * 100, 2) as first_pass_pct, ROUND(CAST(m.NEXT_ITER_SCS_CNT AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(m.NEXT_ITER_SCS_CNT + m.NEXT_ITER_FAIL_CNT, 0) * 100, 2) as retry_pass_pct FROM markets m WHERE m.MARKET = '{state}' ORDER BY m.MONTH DESC
sql_template.ro_level_by_state: SELECT RO_ID, ORG_NM, CNT_STATE, LOB, RUN_NO, rejection_rate, failure_rate, health_score, LATEST_STAGE_NM, FILE_STATUS_CD FROM ros WHERE CNT_STATE = '{state}' ORDER BY RUN_NO DESC, health_score ASC
sql_template.all_states_market: SELECT m.MARKET, m.MONTH, m.FIRST_ITER_SCS_CNT, m.NEXT_ITER_SCS_CNT, m.OVERALL_SCS_CNT, m.SCS_PERCENT, m.retry_lift, ROUND(CAST(m.FIRST_ITER_SCS_CNT AS FLOAT) / NULLIF(m.FIRST_ITER_SCS_CNT + m.FIRST_ITER_FAIL_CNT, 0) * 100, 2) as first_pass_pct FROM markets m ORDER BY m.retry_lift DESC

retry_effectiveness_analysis.interpretation_guidance: Compare first_pass_pct against retry_pass_pct. High retry_lift means retries are recovering failures effectively. Low retry_lift means the root cause is not fixed by reprocessing — investigate rejection reasons instead. If RUN_NO > 3 for an RO with persistent failures, escalate — retries are not helping.
